puzzle.title: The Carrier of Spiteful Fuel
puzzle.intro: In the shadow of a close relation to a prophet, this soul chose darkness over light. Driven by malice, their actions were not merely words of scorn but deeds intended to inflict physical harm, earning a unique and terrible mention in a chapter of the Quran. Who is this figure, forever linked to a fire of their own stoking?
clues: I was connected by marriage to one of the most powerful families in my city.
My spouse was a direct relative of a prophet, yet we were among his most bitter enemies.
I actively participated in the persecution, not content to let my spouse act alone.
I would gather thorny wood and scatter it in the path the prophet would walk at night.
A whole chapter of the Quran was revealed condemning my spouse and me, mentioning us both.
Allah describes me specifically as the 'carrier of firewood' with a rope of palm-fiber around my neck.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Wife of Fir'awn
The Wife of Prophet Lut
The Wife of Abu Lahab
The Wife of Al-'Aziz
Hind bint 'Utbah

trial.label: What Was My Deed?
options: Betraying guests to a wicked town
Scattering thorny wood to harm the Prophet (ﷺ)
Attempting to seduce a righteous servant
Spreading slander against a noble woman
Persuading my spouse to build a tall tower
trial.answer: Scattering thorny wood to harm the Prophet (ﷺ)

location.label: What Was Mentioned With Me?
options: A tray of heavenly food
A staff that turned into a serpent
Garments of pitch
A rope of palm-fiber
A necklace of great value

outcome.label: What Was My Fate?
options: Drowning in the sea with an army
Being swallowed by the earth
Repenting and being accepted into Islam
Being specifically condemned in the Quran to a blazing fire
Being turned into a pillar of salt
outcome.answer: Being specifically condemned in the Quran to a blazing fire

puzzle.verse: And his wife [as well] - the carrier of firewood. (111:4)
